chunks.text: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback: Progress and Challenges - EECS at Berkeley Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback: Progress and Challenges EECS Colloquium Wednesday, April 19, 2023 310 Sutardja Dai Hall (Banatao Auditorium) 5:00 – 6:00 pm Youtube Webinar John Schulman Co-founder, OpenAI John Schulman speaks on "Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback" (4/19/23) Biography John Schulman received a Ph.D. from Berkeley EECS in 2016, advised by Pieter Abbeel. He now leads a team working on ChatGPT and RL from Human Feedback at OpenAI, where he was a co-founder. His previous work includes foundational algorithms of deep RL (TRPO, PPO), generalization in RL (ProcGen), mathematical reasoning by language models (GSM8K), combining RL with retrieval (WebGPT) and studying scaling laws of RL and alignment. In his free time, he enjoys running, jazz piano, and raising chickens.
